title: linaria × sepium var. ochroleuca
intro: Linaria × sepium var. ochroleuca, also known as toadflax or wild toadflax, is a flowering plant that belongs to the figwort family and is a hybrid of Linaria arvensis and Linaria genistifolia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Linaria × sepium var. ochroleuca include its ability to soothe sore throats, calm digestive issues, and reduce inflammation, making it a popular remedy for colds, coughs, and respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
